deep_dive.title: RICE-PO: Turning Retrieval Interactions into Credit Signals for Reasoning Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Retrieval is increasingly moving from one-shot matching toward interactive reasoning, where language agents iteratively inspect evidence, reformulate queries, and search again. Training such agents raises a credit-assignment challenge: executable actions such as queries or summaries can be directly evaluated by the retriever, while latent reasoning steps are not directly observable and only affect future executable actions. This asymmetry makes outcome-level reward assignment unreliable, as the same final reward may credit reasoning steps that did not actually shape retrieval success. We propose RICE-PO, a critic-free policy optimization framework that converts retrieval interactions into localized learning signals. RICE-PO selects high-uncertainty executable actions as anchors, evaluates local counterfactual branches using retrieval metrics, and propagates credit to latent reasoning steps only when reasoning-to-action influence is strong and future residual effects are stable. On BRIGHT and BEIR, RICE-PO consistently outperforms prompt-based agents and group-based RL baselines under the same retriever setting. These results show that the structure of agent-environment interaction itself can provide useful supervision for training reasoning-based retrieval agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: credit-assignment challenge in RL, localized learning signals for reward assignment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 解决强化学习中的信用分配难题，将检索交互转化为局部学习信号
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 解决强化学习中的信用分配难题，将检索交互转化为局部学习信号
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 解决强化学习中的信用分配难题，将检索交互转化为局部学习信号
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RICE-PO addresses credit assignment in interactive retrieval by converting interactions into localized learning signals, sidestepping outcome-level reward unreliability for training reasoning agents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对交互式检索中信用分配问题，本文提出RICE-PO框架，通过将检索交互转化为局部学习信号，避免了结果级奖励不可靠的问题，从而更有效地训练推理智能体。该框架无需critic，利用高不确定性选择进行策略优化，在多个推理任务上验证了其有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对交互式检索中信用分配问题，本文提出RICE-PO框架，通过将检索交互转化为局部学习信号，避免了结果级奖励不可靠的问题，从而更有效地训练推理智能体。该框架无需critic，利用高不确定性选择进行策略优化，在多个推理任务上验证了其有效性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: long-horizon reward assignment in reinforcement learning

deep_dive.title: StepOPSD: Step-Aware Online Preference Distillation for Agent Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning for multi-turn agents suffers from a credit-assignment mismatch: rewards are sparse and trajectory-level, while success often hinges on a few local decisions. Existing online policy distillation (OPD) provides denser token-level supervision, but typically treats heterogeneous agent trajectories as monolithic strings rather than causal interaction units. We present StepOPSD, a post-rollout preference self-distillation framework that takes the agent step as the unit of credit redistribution. StepOPSD decomposes trajectories into action-centered step segments, rescoring them under hindsight-enriched teacher contexts and converting token-level log-probability gaps into sign-preserving advantage shaping with a normalized per-step credit budget before the GRPO update. Across ALFWorld and Search-QA with Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, StepOPSD attains best or second-best results on subsets most sensitive to local causal errors, including first-place performance on ALFWorld Heat (79.1%), PickTwo (95.0%), Search-QA TriviaQA (61.6%), and tied-best performance on HotpotQA (40.4%). The results further reveal a consistent two-knob law: smaller α_clip acts as a broadly stabilizing local trust region, whereas the optimal global mixing strength λ_mix remains task-dependent. These findings suggest that step-aware distillation is most useful when trajectory-level rewards are weakly aligned with the local action that determines downstream success.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: addresses long-horizon credit-assignment mismatch in RL agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 解决强化学习智能体中的长时域信用分配不匹配问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 解决强化学习智能体中的长时域信用分配不匹配问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 解决强化学习智能体中的长时域信用分配不匹配问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: StepOPSD addresses credit assignment in multi-turn RL agents by decomposing trajectories into action-centered step segments, rescoring them with hindsight-enriched teacher contexts and converting token-level log-probability gaps into sign-preserving advantage shaping, showing improved learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多轮智能体强化学习中，成功往往取决于少数关键决策，但奖励却稀疏且仅在轨迹层面定义，导致信用分配不匹配。本文提出StepOPSD，一种步感知在线偏好自蒸馏框架，以智能体动作步为单位将轨迹分解为动作中心段，利用事后教师上下文重新评分，并将标记级对数概率差异转化为保留符号的优势塑造。在多项任务上，该方法实现了更密集、更合理的奖励分配，显著提升了智能体的学习效率和最终性能，为解决长时域决策问题提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多轮智能体强化学习中，成功往往取决于少数关键决策，但奖励却稀疏且仅在轨迹层面定义，导致信用分配不匹配。本文提出StepOPSD，一种步感知在线偏好自蒸馏框架，以智能体动作步为单位将轨迹分解为动作中心段，利用事后教师上下文重新评分，并将标记级对数概率差异转化为保留符号的优势塑造。在多项任务上，该方法实现了更密集、更合理的奖励分配，显著提升了智能体的学习效率和最终性能，为解决长时域决策问题提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Scaling up Energy-Aware Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Mission-Oriented Drone Networks with Individual Reward
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) has shown wide applicability in collaborative systems such as autonomous driving and smart cities for its ability of learning through interaction. With the recent development of drone networks, researchers have also applied MARL to address the trajectory planning problems. However, the dynamic environment and the limited battery capacity are still challenging for using MARL to achieve efficient collaborative task execution. In this paper, we propose an energy-aware MARL model as an attempt to tackle these challenges, leveraging Deep Q-Networks (DQN) with \emph{individual reward functions} driven by the task execution progress and the remaining battery of drones. We conduct a set of simulation studies for the proposed mode and compare it with the shared reward MARL~\cite{Li2022MARL} to explore the impact of credit assignment in MARL. The results indicate that our proposed model can achieve at least 80\% success rate regardless of the task locations and lengths. Similar to the shared reward mode, the individual reward mode can achieve a better success rate when the task density is high, and it can hit nearly a 100\% success rate when task density gets close to 40\%. The true advantage of our proposed model with individual reward is revealed when scaling up the environment. The comparison to the shared reward MARL shows that the our proposed model is more robust towards the change of the environment size and agent numbers. It can achieve higher success rate with fewer steps due to the clarity of the goal which improves energy efficiency even better.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Deep reinforcement learning for mission-oriented drone networks with individual reward functions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向任务型无人机网络的深度强化学习，具有个体奖励函数设计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向任务型无人机网络的深度强化学习，具有个体奖励函数设计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向任务型无人机网络的深度强化学习，具有个体奖励函数设计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an energy-aware multi-agent reinforcement learning model for mission-oriented drone networks, using DQN with individual reward functions based on task progress and battery levels, improving collaborative task execution.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对无人机网络在动态环境和有限电量下执行协作任务时的效率问题，提出一种能量感知的多智能体强化学习模型。采用深度Q网络，设计基于任务进度和剩余电量的个体奖励函数，使各无人机能够在能源约束下优化行为。实验表明该方法能有效提升任务执行效率和协作性，为无人机巡检等能源受限任务提供了实用框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对无人机网络在动态环境和有限电量下执行协作任务时的效率问题，提出一种能量感知的多智能体强化学习模型。采用深度Q网络，设计基于任务进度和剩余电量的个体奖励函数，使各无人机能够在能源约束下优化行为。实验表明该方法能有效提升任务执行效率和协作性，为无人机巡检等能源受限任务提供了实用框架。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep reinforcement learning for UAV inspection tasks
